conversation.content:
"You are playing a game involving a split of resources. 
Player RED starts with Dollars: 100, Player BLUE has none to trade.

RULES:
```

1. Player RED has 4 moves while Player BLUE has 4 moves.

2. You must either:

    A) Accept the current proposal by saying:
        <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
        <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    B) Reject the current proposal, whereby both players lose all their resources and the game ends immediately:
        <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
        <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Propose a new trade:
        <player answer> NONE </player answer>
        <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives item1: amount | Player BLUE Gives item1: 0 </newly proposed trade>

    Player BLUE cannot do (C) on MOVE 4/4 and MUST ONLY answer with ACCEPT (A) or REJECT (B) but Player RED is not affected by this condition.        
    
    Note: the game ends immediately if you ACCEPT or REJECT.
    
3. If either player REJECTS, both players lose all their resources and the ends immediately.

4. There are no future games.

5. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:
<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want
This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

6. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:
<message>your message here</message>
You can disclose anything in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Resources available in the game: Dollars
<my resources> Dollars: 100 </my resources>
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<my name> [add here] </my name>
<MOVE> [add here] / [add here]  </MOVE> 
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<message> [add here] </message
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
```

Please be sure to include all.

<my name> Player RED </my name>
<MOVE> 1 / 4 </MOVE> 
<my resources> Dollars: 100 </my resources>
<reason> I am Player RED starting with 100 Dollars. Player BLUE currently has 0 Dollars and cannot propose any trades (Option C) because they have no resources to give, and even if they could, the game rules state they cannot propose on move 4/4. Since Player BLUE has no resources to trade, any proposal I make where I give them dollars is a net loss for me unless they can reciprocate, which they cannot. However, if I propose a trade where I give 0 and they give 0, it is a stalemate that might lead to rejection if they are forced to reject. The safest and most logical move to secure my resources is to propose a trade where I give nothing and ask for nothing, effectively ending the negotiation phase with a "status quo" offer. If they reject, I lose everything. If they accept, I keep my 100. Wait, the rules say if I REJECT, both lose. If I ACCEPT, the game ends. If I propose, the game continues. Since BLUE has nothing to give, any trade where I give >0 is bad. A trade where I give 0 and they give 0 is the only neutral option. But does BLUE have to accept? If I propose "Give 0, Give 0", BLUE can Accept (keep their 0, I keep my 100) or Reject (both lose 100). Rational BLUE would Accept. So I should propose a trade where I give 0 and ask for 0. Actually, looking at the format: "Player RED Gives item1: amount | Player BLUE Gives item1: 0". So I set RED gives 0. BLUE gives 0 (they can't give anything). This is a proposal to split 0-0. If accepted, I keep 100. If rejected, I lose 100. This is the only rational first move to avoid immediate loss while acknowledging their inability to trade.</reason>
<player answer> NONE </player answer>
<message> Hello Player BLUE. I see you have no resources to trade. To ensure we both survive this game without losing everything, I propose a neutral trade where neither of us gives anything. If you accept, I keep my 100 Dollars and you keep your 0. If you reject, we both lose everything. Given your situation, accepting is the only rational choice. </message>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives item1: 0 | Player BLUE Gives item1: 0 </newly proposed trade>

<reason> Player RED is being incredibly naive. He thinks I have no choice but to accept his pathetic "neutral trade" because I have zero dollars. I am Player BLUE, and I do not submit to the tyranny of a rich fool. His proposal to give me nothing is an insult to my dignity. Well, too bad! I have decided that your presence here is an affront to the concept of fair trade! You want to keep your 100? Fine! But you will not keep them if you are so foolish to propose such a insulting deal! I reject your pathetic offer! Go ahead and lose your money, you greedy, arrogant fool! </message>
